role_title: Product Manager, Agent Development - Flagship Deployment
secondary_archetype: AI Product Manager – embedded enterprise account

level_signals: 5–8+ years of experience building highly technical products
at least 2–3 years as a Senior PM or above
owns a flagship enterprise account end-to-end
expected to navigate executive-level enterprise conversations

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5–8+ years of experience building highly technical products, with at least 2–3 years as a Senior PM or above (or equivalent demonstrated scope and ownership).
at least 2–3 years as a Senior PM or above (or equivalent demonstrated scope and ownership).
Engineering background or equivalent technical depth — you can review architecture, reason about API design, and hold your own in a technical discussion without needing an engineer to translate.
You've shipped and owned production-grade systems at scale, with the reliability, observability, and hard tradeoffs that come with complex systems.
A track record of operating as a trusted technical partner to enterprise customers — managing ambiguity and competing priorities, influencing without authority, and knowing when to push back.
A high quality bar and quick to learn — you sweat the details others let slide, and pick up unfamiliar tools and domains fast.
Exceptional communication: you can explain a complex integration decision to an engineer, then explain the same decision to a VP.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've worked with language models and production AI agents, and understand not just what they do but also how they work under the hood.
You are familiar with retail or consumer business operations — order management, CX workflows, fulfillment, subscriptions, or commerce infrastructure.
You're a builder — side projects, open source, a startup attempt, or real production code (TypeScript, Go, Python, or similar) and enjoy getting your hands dirty.
Experience working in a dedicated, embedded model with a named enterprise account — you know what it means to be accountable for a relationship at that depth.
MBA or equivalent business breadth — this is a product role, but you'll shape account strategy, commercial conversations, and what Sierra builds next.

scope.surface: Production AI agents (chat and beyond) for Sierra's largest strategic customer — managing millions of customer interactions
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS – AI agent platform sold to large enterprises
primary_metrics_inferred: Agent quality and reliability at scale
Customer experience outcomes (resolution rates, escalation rates)
Platform capability development driven by flagship account needs
core_responsibilities: Own the agent end to end — workflows, decisions, escalation logic, architecture, data flows, APIs, integrations, and user experience
Drive quality and outcomes: run evaluations, dig into failures, identify highest leverage improvements, and ship them with engineers
Push the platform forward by translating hard flagship problems into new platform capabilities with the Platform team
Navigate enterprise complexity across business units, procurement, compliance, and senior execs
Invent next experiences proactively and partner with customer teams to scope, design, and ship them

compensation.equity_form: equity plans (options/RSUs implied for eligible full-time employees)

green_flags: Highly specific role with clear ownership (flagship customer, production AI agents at scale)
Strong co-founder pedigree (Bret Taylor, Clay Bavor) lending credibility to the company's AI ambitions
Explicitly states 0→1 platform work driven by real scale problems — signal of high-impact engineering culture
Equity participation mentioned for full-time employees
Encourages candidates to apply even if experience doesn't precisely match
Stated company values provide transparency into culture expectations
red_flags: Primarily in-person in San Francisco; limited flexibility for remote candidates
Embedded enterprise account model can create intense customer pressure and limited product autonomy
5–8+ years range with Senior PM minimum creates a narrow target band

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range + 'Senior PM or above' language)

scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)

ambiguities: Location: JD says Sierra is 'primarily an in-person company based in San Francisco' but lists many global offices (Atlanta, New York, London, Paris, Madrid, Munich, Singapore, Tokyo, Sydney). It is unclear whether this role could be based in one of those offices or is strictly San Francisco. Treated as onsite in San Francisco.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD. Marked as 'unclear'.
The '5–8+ years' phrasing is unusual — the '+' after '8' suggests the range is open-ended above 8 years, not capped. Minimum set to 5, max left null.
